questions.solutionText: Solution Radio waves are in the lowest frequency range of the electromagnetic spectrum. Above that, we have infrared, visible, ultraviolet, X-rays and gamma rays. Like all other electromagnetic waves, radio waves also travel with the speed of light. The wavelength of radio waves is in the range of 1 mm to 100 km . Naturally occurring radio waves are produced by lightning or by astronomical objects. Artificially generated radio waves are used for communication.

questions.solutionText: Solution Let's assume, Angle of incidence to be i , Angle of emergence to be e , Angle of prism to be A , Angle of deviation as δ and Refractive index of the prism, μ . Applying the relation between the angles and refractive index of a prism, i + e - A = δ = ( μ - 1 ) A . A = 4 ° , δ = 2 ° , e = 0 (Emergent ray is normal to the surface). On substituting these values, we get, i + 0 - 4 ° = 3 2 - 1 4 ° i + 0 - 4 ° = 2 ° . ⇒ i = 6 ° .

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Let's assume,

Angle of incidence to be i,

Angle of emergence to be e,

Angle of prism to be A,

Angle of deviation as δ and

Refractive index of the prism, μ.

Applying the relation between the angles and refractive index of a prism,

i+e-A = δ = (μ-1)A.

A = 4°,δ =2°,

e=0(Emergent ray is normal to the surface).

On substituting these values, we get,

i +0-4°=32-14°

i +0-4°=2°.

⇒i =6°.

questions.solutionText: Solution When similar poles are on same side time period of oscillation T 1 is given by T 1 = 2 π I 1 + I 2 m 1 + m 2 B H [Given] m 1 = 2 m , m 2 = m T 1 = 2 π I 1 + I 2 3 m B H ...(i) When the polarity of magnet is reversed, time period of oscillation T 2 is given by. T 2 = 2 π I 1 + I 2 m 1 - m 2 B H T 2 = 2 π I 1 + I 2 m B H On comparing equations (i) and (ii), we get T 2 > T 1 or T 1 < T 2 ^

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
When similar poles are on same side time period of oscillation T1 is given by
T1=2πI1+I2m1+m2BH [Given]
m1=2m, m2=m
T1=2πI1+I23mBH ...(i)
When the polarity of magnet is reversed, time period of oscillation T2 is given by.
T2=2πI1+I2m1-m2BH
T2=2πI1+I2mBH
On comparing equations (i) and (ii), we get
T2>T1
or T1<T2

questions.solutionText: Solution From Gauss's Law we know that, ϕ = q i n ε 0 where, q i n is the net charge enclosed So, for the given surface net charge enclosed can be calculated, The total charge enclosed in the surface A is, q i n = q 1 + q 2 + q 3 = - 14 + 78.85 - 56 n C ⇒ q in = 8 . 85 × 10 - 9 C Total flux through the surface A is, ϕ = q i n ε 0 = 8.85 × 10 - 9 8.85 × 10 - 12 = 10 3 N m 2 C - 1 Therefore electric flux for the gaussian surface encloses the charged particle is free space is 10 3 N m 2 C - 1 . !

From Gauss's Law we know that,

ϕ=qinε0

questions.solutionText: Solution Besides EO 2 type, Sulphur, Selenium, and tellurium also form EO 3 type oxides. Group 16 Elements: Oxygen and Sulfur are non-metals, Selenium and Tellurium are metalloids and Polonium is a metal under typical conditions. All the elements of this group need 2 electrons to attain inert gas configuration either by gaining or sharing of electrons. Oxygen has high E.N so it gains two electrons to become inert gas configuration so all metal oxides are ionic and contains O 2 − in which the oxidation state is -2 . Oxygen shows − 1 , − 1 / 2 , + 1 and +2 oxidation state. The tendency to show -2 oxidation state decreases from sulphur to polonium.

questions.questionHtml: Standard entropies of X2, Y2 and XY3 are 60, 40 and 50 J K−1mol−1 respectively. For the reaction 12X2+32Y2⇌XY3, ΔH=−30 kJ to be at equilibrium, the temperature should be:
questions.solutionText: Solution So from this question we know that enthalpy for the reaction is - 30 KJ 1 2 X 2 + 3 2 Y 2 → XY 3 ∆ H = - 30 KJ So we know from the concept of standard entropies is ∆ S = ∆ S P - ∆ S R ∆ S = ∆ S XY 3 - 3 2 ∆ S Y 2 - 1 2 ∆ S X 2 ∆ S = 50 - 3 2 × 40 - 1 2 × 60 ∆ S = - 40 JK - 1 mol - 1 So according to the second law of thermodynamics ∆ G = ∆ H - T ∆ S For equilibrium the free Gibb's energy is ∆ G = 0 ∆ H = T ∆ S T = ∆ H ∆ S T = - 30 × 10 3 J - 40 JK - 1 So the value of temperature is T = 750 K
questions.solution: Solution So from this question we know that enthalpy for the reaction is - 30 KJ 1 2 X 2 + 3 2 Y 2 → XY 3 ∆ H = - 30 KJ So we know from the concept of standard entropies is ∆ S = ∆ S P - ∆ S R ∆ S = ∆ S XY 3 - 3 2 ∆ S Y 2 - 1 2 ∆ S X 2 ∆ S = 50 - 3 2 × 40 - 1 2 × 60 ∆ S = - 40 JK - 1 mol - 1 So according to the second law of thermodynamics ∆ G = ∆ H - T ∆ S For equilibrium the free Gibb's energy is ∆ G = 0 ∆ H = T ∆ S T = ∆ H ∆ S T = - 30 × 10 3 J - 40 JK - 1 So the value of temperature is T = 750 K
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

So from this question we know that enthalpy for the reaction is -30 KJ

12X2 + 32Y2 → XY3 ∆H = -30 KJ

So we know from the concept of standard entropies is

∆S = ∆SP - ∆SR
∆S = ∆SXY3 -32 ∆SY2-12∆SX2
∆S = 50 - 32×40-12×60
∆S = -40 JK-1mol-1

So according to the second law of thermodynamics

∆G = ∆H-T∆S

For equilibrium the free Gibb's energy is ∆G = 0

∆H = T∆S
T = ∆H∆S
T = -30×103J-40JK-1

So the value of temperature is

T = 750 K

questions.solutionText: Solution The underlined part has a subject-verb agreement error. "United Nations" is a singular entity, so the verb should be "touches" (not "touch"). Option (1): "in which the United Nations touches the lives of people everywhere" — corrects the verb to "touches" while keeping the rest intact. Option (2): uses "which" without "in" — changes meaning. Option (3): adds unnecessary words "all the" and "in every place" — redundant. Option (4): drops "the" before "United Nations" — grammatically incorrect.

questions.questionHtml:
A sentence is given followed by a blank. You are provided with three fragments I, II and III. You have to identify which statement/statements can carry forward the given sentence in the most logical way so as to make the sentence coherent and contextually correct.

On one hand, the government is providing ____________________ .

I. power to industrialists of Vidarbha and Marathwada at concessional rates but on the other, the State Discom MSEDCL is providing free electricity to them.

II. cheap food to people and on the other, it is increasing fuel prices.

III. water and power supply in some colonies but on the other side, government has lifted the ban on their development.
questions.solutionText: Solution I. On one hand, the government is providing power to industrialists of Vidarbha and Marathwada at concessional rates but on the other, the State Discom MSEDCL is providing free electricity to them. Whenever we have the expression ‘on one hand..on the other hand..’, we have two contradicting statements for each. Here, both statements agree with each other and hence, the sentence is not logical. II. On one hand, the government is providing cheap food to people and on the other, it is increasing fuel prices. This is correct. III. On one hand, the government is providing water and power supply in some colonies but on the other side, government has lifted the ban on their development. Here too, we have two statements agreeing with one another and hence illogical. Thus, option A is correct.

II. On one hand, the government is providing cheap food to people and on the other, it is increasing fuel prices. This is correct. III. On one hand, the government is providing water and power supply in some colonies but on the other side, government has lifted the ban on their development. Here too, we have two statements agreeing with one another and hence illogical. Thus, option A is correct.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
I. On one hand, the government is providing power to industrialists of Vidarbha and Marathwada at concessional rates but on the other, the State Discom MSEDCL is providing free electricity to them.

Whenever we have the expression ‘on one hand..on the other hand..’, we have two contradicting statements for each. Here, both statements agree with each other and hence, the sentence is not logical.
II. On one hand, the government is providing cheap food to people and on the other, it is increasing fuel prices. This is correct.
III. On one hand, the government is providing water and power supply in some colonies but on the other side, government has lifted the ban on their development.

Here too, we have two statements agreeing with one another and hence illogical.

questions.solutionText: Solution Sentence C says "The cyclist from the main road did not notice it." — the word "it" must refer to something that the cyclist failed to notice, which then caused the accident. Option (4): "A speeding car came from a side road" — this is something the cyclist on the main road could fail to notice, logically leading to the accident described in A. questions.solutionText: Solution To find the closest meaning, we have to look at the definitions of the words: Gesticulation: This refers to the act of using hand, arm, or body movements (gestures) to express an idea or emphasize what is being said. Pantomime: This is the art or technique of conveying emotions, actions, or feelings through gestures and body movements without using speech. Because both words fundamentally deal with using physical body movements to communicate meaning, "Pantomime" is the closest synonym among the choices. Here is why the other options are incorrect: (1) Oratory: This is the art of formal public speaking. It relies almost entirely on vocal words, making it the opposite of gesticulation. (2) Mimicry: This is the act of imitating someone or something (like a parrot mimicking a voice). While it can involve movement, its core definition is imitation, not just general communication through gestures. (4) Articulation: This refers to the physical production of clear speech sounds or the clear expression of ideas in words. Again, it is primarily verbal rather than physical.

To find the closest meaning, we have to look at the definitions of the words:

Gesticulation: This refers to the act of using hand, arm, or body movements (gestures) to express an idea or emphasize what is being said.

Pantomime: This is the art or technique of conveying emotions, actions, or feelings through gestures and body movements without using speech.

Because both words fundamentally deal with using physical body movements to communicate meaning, "Pantomime" is the closest synonym among the choices.

Here is why the other options are incorrect:

(1) Oratory: This is the art of formal public speaking. It relies almost entirely on vocal words, making it the opposite of gesticulation.

(2) Mimicry: This is the act of imitating someone or something (like a parrot mimicking a voice). While it can involve movement, its core definition is imitation, not just general communication through gestures.

(4) Articulation: This refers to the physical production of clear speech sounds or the clear expression of ideas in words. Again, it is primarily verbal rather than physical.

questions.questionHtml: A man walks 15 m towards South from a fixed point. From there he goes 12 m towards North and then 4 m towards West. How far and in what direction is he from the fixed point?
questions.solutionText: Solution According to the given information, the following line diagram can be drawn for the given directions: Here, O is the starting point and C is the ending point for the man. OB = 15 m AB = 12 m OB = OA + AB ∴ OA = OB - AB OA = 15 - 12 OA = 3 m ∴ OC = AC 2 + OA 2 OC = 4 2 + 3 2 OC = 16 + 9 OC = 25 OC = 5 m Hence, he is 5 m far and in the South - West direction from his starting point. So, the correct option is (C).

questions.solutionText: Solution Statement (I): N is both a perfect square and a perfect cube, so N is a perfect sixth power. N = k 6 for some integer k . With 1 < N < 93 : k = 2 ⇒ N = 64 . ( k = 1 gives N = 1 , excluded; k = 3 gives 729 > 93 ). So N = 64 . Statement (I) alone is sufficient. Statement (II): N is divisible by 8, so N = 8 m , i.e., N = 64 m 2 . With 1 < N < 93 : m = 1 ⇒ N = 64 . ( m = 2 gives 256 > 93 ). So N = 64 . Statement (II) alone is sufficient.

( k = 1 gives N = 1 , excluded; k = 3 gives 729 > 93 ). So N = 64 . Statement (I) alone is sufficient. Statement (II): N is divisible by 8, so N = 8 m , i.e., N = 64 m 2 . With 1 < N < 93 : m = 1 ⇒ N = 64 . ( m = 2 gives 256 > 93 ). So N = 64 . Statement (II) alone is sufficient.

Solution
Statement (I): N is both a perfect square and a perfect cube, so N is a perfect sixth power.

N=k6 for some integer k. With 1<N<93: k=2⇒N=64. (k=1 gives N=1, excluded; k=3 gives 729>93).

So N=64. Statement (I) alone is sufficient.

Statement (II): N is divisible by 8, so N=8m, i.e., N=64m2.

With 1<N<93: m=1⇒N=64. (m=2 gives 256>93).

So N=64. Statement (II) alone is sufficient.

questions.solutionText: Solution 6 students: Jaya, Amitabh, Abhishek, Aishwarya, Salman, Vivek. Two teams of 3 each. Constraints: - Jaya and Amitabh in different teams - Abhishek and Aishwarya in the same team - Salman and Vivek in different teams Abhishek and Aishwarya take 2 spots in one team. That team needs 1 more member. Jaya and Amitabh are in different teams, Salman and Vivek are in different teams. Team with Abhishek + Aishwarya needs 1 from {Jaya, Amitabh, Salman, Vivek}. The other team gets the remaining 3. Regardless of how the teams are formed, Abhishek and Aishwarya are always selected together and must be in the same team.

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
6 students: Jaya, Amitabh, Abhishek, Aishwarya, Salman, Vivek. Two teams of 3 each.

Constraints:
- Jaya and Amitabh in different teams
- Abhishek and Aishwarya in the same team
- Salman and Vivek in different teams

Abhishek and Aishwarya take 2 spots in one team. That team needs 1 more member.

Jaya and Amitabh are in different teams, Salman and Vivek are in different teams.

Team with Abhishek + Aishwarya needs 1 from {Jaya, Amitabh, Salman, Vivek}. The other team gets the remaining 3.

Regardless of how the teams are formed, Abhishek and Aishwarya are always selected together and must be in the same team.

It is clear from above that the ages of W and T are the same.

questions.solutionText: Solution Statements: S1: Some applicants are examiners. S2: All invigilators are examiners. S3: Some students are applicants. I. "At least some invigilators being applicants is a possibility" — No rule prevents invigilators and applicants from overlapping. → Follows. II. "All students being examiners is a possibility" — Nothing prevents all students from being examiners in a valid Venn diagram. → Follows. III. "Some applicants are not students" — This is a definite claim, but we can draw a valid diagram where all applicants are students. So it doesn't necessarily follow. Answer: (1) Only I and II follow

S2: All invigilators are examiners.
S3: Some students are applicants.

III. "Some applicants are not students" — This is a definite claim, but we can draw a valid diagram where all applicants are students. So it doesn't necessarily follow.

Answer: (1) Only I and II follow

questions.solutionText: Solution No coke is sprite: This means the circle for "Coke" and the circle for "Sprite" cannot touch or overlap at all. No pepsi is coke: This means the circle for "Pepsi" and the circle for "Coke" cannot touch or overlap. Conclusion I: No sprite is pepsi. While it's possible they don't overlap, it's not certain. Logic requires a conclusion to be true in every possible scenario. Since Pepsi and Sprite could overlap (or one could even be inside the other) without touching Coke, we cannot say for sure that "No sprite is pepsi." So, does not follow. Conclusion II: All pepsi are sprite. Again, there is no statement that forces Pepsi to be inside the Sprite circle. They could be completely separate. So, does not follow. Answer: (4) Neither conclusion I or conclusion II follows

questions.solutionText: Solution n M = 23 , n P = 24 , n C = 19 n M ∩ P = 12 , n M ∩ C = 9 , n P ∩ C = 7 n M ∩ P ∩ C = 4 We have to find n M ∩ P ′ ∩ C ′ , n P ∩ M ′ ∩ C ′ , n C ∩ M ′ ∩ P ′ Now n M ∩ P ′ ∩ C ′ = n M ∩ P ∪ C ′ = n M - n M ∩ P ∪ C = n M - n M ∩ P ∪ M ∩ C = n M - n M ∩ P - n M ∩ C + n M ∩ P ∩ C = 23 - 12 - 9 + 4 = 27 - 21 = 6 n P ∩ M ′ ∩ C ′ = n P ∩ M ∪ C ′ = n P - n P ∩ M ∪ C = n P - n P ∩ M ∪ P ∩ C = n P - n P ∩ M - n P ∩ C + n P ∩ M ∩ C = 24 - 12 - 7 + 4 = 9 n C ∩ M ′ ∩ P ′ = n C - n C ∩ P - n C ∩ M + n C ∩ P ∩ M = 19 - 7 - 9 + 4 = 23 - 16 = 7 . Hence, the total no. of students studying exactly one subject are 6 + 9 + 7 = 22
questions.solution: Solution n M = 23 , n P = 24 , n C = 19 n M ∩ P = 12 , n M ∩ C = 9 , n P ∩ C = 7 n M ∩ P ∩ C = 4 We have to find n M ∩ P ′ ∩ C ′ , n P ∩ M ′ ∩ C ′ , n C ∩ M ′ ∩ P ′ Now n M ∩ P ′ ∩ C ′ = n M ∩ P ∪ C ′ = n M - n M ∩ P ∪ C = n M - n M ∩ P ∪ M ∩ C = n M - n M ∩ P - n M ∩ C + n M ∩ P ∩ C = 23 - 12 - 9 + 4 = 27 - 21 = 6 n P ∩ M ′ ∩ C ′ = n P ∩ M ∪ C ′ = n P - n P ∩ M ∪ C = n P - n P ∩ M ∪ P ∩ C = n P - n P ∩ M - n P ∩ C + n P ∩ M ∩ C = 24 - 12 - 7 + 4 = 9 n C ∩ M ′ ∩ P ′ = n C - n C ∩ P - n C ∩ M + n C ∩ P ∩ M = 19 - 7 - 9 + 4 = 23 - 16 = 7 . Hence, the total no. of students studying exactly one subject are 6 + 9 + 7 = 22

nM=23, nP=24, nC=19
nM∩ P=12, nM∩C=9, nP∩C=7

We have to find nM∩P′∩C′, nP∩M′∩C′ ,
nC∩M′∩P′
Now nM∩P′∩C′=nM ∩P∪C′
= nM-nM∩P ∪ C
=nM-nM∩P∪M∩C
=nM-nM∩P-nM∩C+nM∩P∩C
=23-12-9+4=27-21=6
nP∩ M′∩ C′= nP∩ M∪ C′
= nP- nP∩ M∪ C= nP-nP∩M∪P∩C
= nP-nP∩M-nP∩C+nP∩M∩C
= 24 - 12 - 7 + 4 = 9
nC∩M′∩P′=nC-nC∩P-nC∩M+nC∩P∩M

questions.solutionText: Solution Given x 3 d y + x y · d x = x 2 d y + 2 y d x ⇒ x 3 - x 2 d y = 2 - x y d x ⇒ d y y = 2 - x x 3 - x 2 d x Integrating both sides with respect to x , we get ∫ d y y = ∫ 2 - x x 2 x - 1 d x + k . . . . . . . . . . . . i Let 2 - x x 2 x - 1 = A x + B x 2 + C x - 1 ⇒ 2 - x = A x x - 1 + B x - 1 + C x 2 Putting x = 0 ⇒ 2 = - B ⇒ B = - 2 Putting x = 1 ⇒ 2 - 1 = C ⇒ C = 1 Putting x = 2 ⇒ 2 - 2 = A 2 1 + B 1 + C 2 2 ⇒ 2 A + 2 = 0 ⇒ A = - 1 From equation i , we get ∫ d y y = ∫ - 1 x + - 2 x 2 + 1 x - 1 d x + k ⇒ ln y = - ln x + 2 x + ln x - 1 + k . . . . . . . . . . i i Given y 2 = e i.e. at x = 2 , y = e ⇒ ln e = - ln 2 + 2 2 + ln 2 - 1 + k ⇒ 1 = - ln 2 + 1 + 0 + k ⇒ k = ln 2 Putting in equation i i , we get ln y = - ln x + 2 x + ln x - 1 + ln 2 Now putting x = 4 , we get ln y = - ln 4 + 2 4 + ln 4 - 1 + ln 2 ⇒ ln y = - 2 ln 2 + 1 2 + ln 3 + ln 2 ⇒ ln y = - ln 2 + 1 2 + ln 3 ⇒ ln y = 1 2 + ln 3 2 ⇒ ln 2 y 3 = 1 2 ⇒ 2 y 3 = e 1 2 ⇒ y = 3 2 e ⇒ y 4 = 3 2 e
questions.solution: Solution Given x 3 d y + x y · d x = x 2 d y + 2 y d x ⇒ x 3 - x 2 d y = 2 - x y d x ⇒ d y y = 2 - x x 3 - x 2 d x Integrating both sides with respect to x , we get ∫ d y y = ∫ 2 - x x 2 x - 1 d x + k . . . . . . . . . . . . i Let 2 - x x 2 x - 1 = A x + B x 2 + C x - 1 ⇒ 2 - x = A x x - 1 + B x - 1 + C x 2 Putting x = 0 ⇒ 2 = - B ⇒ B = - 2 Putting x = 1 ⇒ 2 - 1 = C ⇒ C = 1 Putting x = 2 ⇒ 2 - 2 = A 2 1 + B 1 + C 2 2 ⇒ 2 A + 2 = 0 ⇒ A = - 1 From equation i , we get ∫ d y y = ∫ - 1 x + - 2 x 2 + 1 x - 1 d x + k ⇒ ln y = - ln x + 2 x + ln x - 1 + k . . . . . . . . . . i i Given y 2 = e i.e. at x = 2 , y = e ⇒ ln e = - ln 2 + 2 2 + ln 2 - 1 + k ⇒ 1 = - ln 2 + 1 + 0 + k ⇒ k = ln 2 Putting in equation i i , we get ln y = - ln x + 2 x + ln x - 1 + ln 2 Now putting x = 4 , we get ln y = - ln 4 + 2 4 + ln 4 - 1 + ln 2 ⇒ ln y = - 2 ln 2 + 1 2 + ln 3 + ln 2 ⇒ ln y = - ln 2 + 1 2 + ln 3 ⇒ ln y = 1 2 + ln 3 2 ⇒ ln 2 y 3 = 1 2 ⇒ 2 y 3 = e 1 2 ⇒ y = 3 2 e ⇒ y 4 = 3 2 e

a=2

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Since f(x)=3sin⁡x−cos⁡x−2ax+b is decreasing for all real values of x, therefore f(x)<0 for all x.
⇒3cos⁡x+sin⁡x−2a<0 for all x
⇒32cos⁡x+12sin⁡x<a for all x

questions.questionHtml:
Let f:[1,3]→R be a function satisfying x[x]≤f(x)≤6−x, for all x≠2 and f(2)=1, where R is the set of all real numbers and [x] denotes the largest integer less than or equal to x.

questions.solutionText: Solution Consider x [ x ] ≤ f ( x ) ≤ 6 − x ⇒ lim x → 2 − x [ x ] = 2 1 = 2 ⇒ lim x → 2 − 6 − x = 2 ∴ lim x → 2 − f ( x ) = 2 [By Sandwich theorem] Now lim x → 2 + x [ x ] = 1 , lim x → 2 + 6 − x = 2 Hence by Sandwich theorem lim x → 2 + f ( x ) does not exists. Therefore f is not continuous at x = 2 . Thus statement-1 is true but statement- 2 is not true

questions.solutionText: Solution We are given: d y d x − tan ⁡ y x = tan ⁡ y sin ⁡ y x 2 d y d x = tan ⁡ y x + tan ⁡ y sin ⁡ y x 2 = tan ⁡ y ( 1 x + sin ⁡ y x 2 ) Now consider the option (a): x sin ⁡ y + log ⁡ x = C Differentiate both sides: d d x ( x sin ⁡ y + log ⁡ x ) = 0 Apply derivative: d d x ( x sin ⁡ y ) + 1 x = sin ⁡ y ⋅ 1 − x cos ⁡ y ⋅ d y d x sin 2 ⁡ y + 1 x = 0 Multiply both sides by sin 2 ⁡ y : sin ⁡ y − x cos ⁡ y ⋅ d y d x + sin 2 ⁡ y x = 0 ⇒ d y d x = tan ⁡ y x + tan ⁡ y sin ⁡ y x 2 Which matches the given equation, hence: Final Answer: (a) x sin ⁡ y + log ⁡ x = C
questions.solution: Solution We are given: d y d x − tan ⁡ y x = tan ⁡ y sin ⁡ y x 2 d y d x = tan ⁡ y x + tan ⁡ y sin ⁡ y x 2 = tan ⁡ y ( 1 x + sin ⁡ y x 2 ) Now consider the option (a): x sin ⁡ y + log ⁡ x = C Differentiate both sides: d d x ( x sin ⁡ y + log ⁡ x ) = 0 Apply derivative: d d x ( x sin ⁡ y ) + 1 x = sin ⁡ y ⋅ 1 − x cos ⁡ y ⋅ d y d x sin 2 ⁡ y + 1 x = 0 Multiply both sides by sin 2 ⁡ y : sin ⁡ y − x cos ⁡ y ⋅ d y d x + sin 2 ⁡ y x = 0 ⇒ d y d x = tan ⁡ y x + tan ⁡ y sin ⁡ y x 2 Which matches the given equation, hence: Final Answer: (a) x sin ⁡ y + log ⁡ x = C
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
We are given:
dydx−tan⁡yx=tan⁡ysin⁡yx2
dydx=tan⁡yx+tan⁡ysin⁡yx2=tan⁡y(1x+sin⁡yx2)
Now consider the option (a):
xsin⁡y+log⁡x=C
Differentiate both sides:
ddx(xsin⁡y+log⁡x)=0
Apply derivative:
ddx(xsin⁡y)+1x=sin⁡y⋅1−xcos⁡y⋅dydxsin2⁡y+1x=0
Multiply both sides by sin2⁡y :
sin⁡y−xcos⁡y⋅dydx+sin2⁡yx=0⇒dydx=tan⁡yx+tan⁡ysin⁡yx2
Which matches the given equation, hence:
Final Answer: (a) xsin⁡y+log⁡x=C

questions.solutionText: Solution Since a → + b → + c → = 0 → , the three vectors form a triangle with sides 2, 5, 29 . So this is a right triangle with the right angle between sides a → and b → . In this triangle, the angles opposite to sides | a → | = 2 , | b → | = 5 , | c → | = 29 are A , B , C respectively, with C = 90 ° . Now θ 1 (angle between a → and c → ) is the exterior angle supplement: θ 1 = π − A θ 2 (angle between b → and c → ) is: θ 2 = π − B θ 1 + θ 2 = 2 π − ( A + B ) = 2 π − π 2 = 3 π 2 (Since A + B = π 2 because C = 90 ° )
